title: Understand: The Compromises at the Convention: The Great Compromise and the Electoral College
description: Check your understanding of the compromises at the Convention in this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: The Road to Reform, the Constitutional Convention, and the Battle for Ratification
The Compromises at the Convention: The Great Compromise and the Electoral College
Understand: The Compromises at the Convention: The Great Compromise and the Electoral College

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the phrases that best complete the sentence.**

In the debates over representation in Congress, the large states favored the proportional representation of the \_\_\_\_, while the smaller states favored equal representation proposed by the \_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Virginia Plan; New Jersey Plan
New Jersey Plan; Virginia Plan
British government; French government
Articles of Confederation; Declaration of Independence

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the result of the Connecticut Compromise?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All states would have equal representation in both the House of Representatives and the Senate.
Every state would have the same number of members in the House of Representatives.
The House of Representatives would be based on proportional representation, while the Senate would have equal state representation.
Connecticut would have the most representatives, while other states would have only two representatives.

assessment_items.question_content:
**After much debate about how to elect the president of the United States, what system did the delegates at the Constitutional Convention agree on?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president would be chosen by direct popular vote.
The president would be chosen by members of Congress.
The president would be chosen by state legislatures.
The president would be chosen by the Electoral College.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What decisions did the delegates at the Constitutional Convention need to make about the presidency of the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: How long the president’s term should be
Whether the president should be allowed to run for reelection
How to remove a president from office
Which political party the president should be from

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following was a goal of the Founding generation at the Constitutional Convention?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Striking a balance between federal power and individual liberty
Creating a plan to end slavery in the United States
Revising all of the laws that had been passed by the states after the American Revolution
Redrawing boundary lines between the states to make sure that each had the same population

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did William Paterson and his allies oppose the Virginia Plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They worried that it would give too much power to the states with the largest populations.
They thought it was too similar to the government under the Articles of Confederation.
They feared that it would lead to the end of slavery in America.
They wanted the national legislature to have the power to veto state laws.

assessment_items.question_content:
**At the Constitutional Convention, which proposed plan most strengthened the powers of the national government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The New Jersey Plan
The Virginia Plan
The Marshall Plan
The Georgia Plan

assessment_items.question_content:
**During the Constitutional Convention, the delegates set out to create a new national government that was \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: weaker than state governments, so that the national government had to follow the authority of the states
powerful enough to defeat the British military and secure American independence from British rule
responsible for prosecuting state governments for violating the civil liberties of their citizens
strong enough to address local uprisings and achieve goals that affected all the states

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which issue did the Great Compromise address?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rise of political parties in politics
The establishment of a single executive
The creation of a supreme court
The number of representatives in Congress

assessment_items.question_content:
**The United States is unique among democratic nations because the president of the United States is \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: approved by a vote of sitting state governors
appointed by members of the Senate
elected directly by the people
elected indirectly through the Electoral College

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which framer opposed the development of a strong executive branch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: John Dickinson
James Wilson
Roger Sherman
Alexander Hamilton

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes why some delegates from the southern states supported the Electoral College?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president would need to win an electoral vote from each state to win the election, ensuring that the southern states would have more power over elections than northern states.
The Electoral College would ensure that all states had the same number of electoral votes, weakening the influence of cities with high populations.
The number of electoral votes was partly based on population, and the Three-Fifths Compromise guaranteed that slaveholding states would have more voting power.
The Electoral College would screen and assess any candidates for the presidency and would only put forward candidates who met moral qualifications.
